source_pdf: toefl primary classify words new new G6.pdf

entries.englishDefinition: To have the same idea or say yes to something.
entries.exampleSentence: I agree with your answer in class today.

entries.englishDefinition: To tell people something in a clear or official way.
entries.exampleSentence: The teacher will announce the news after lunch.

entries.englishDefinition: To get to a place.
entries.exampleSentence: We arrive at school at eight o'clock.

entries.englishDefinition: To say sorry for something.
entries.exampleSentence: He apologized for being late to music class.

entries.englishDefinition: To make something curve or not straight.
entries.exampleSentence: Please bend down to pick up the pencil.

entries.englishDefinition: To take and use something that you will give back later.
entries.exampleSentence: I borrow books from the library every Friday.

entries.englishDefinition: To jump back after hitting a surface.
entries.exampleSentence: The ball will bounce back from the wall.

entries.englishDefinition: To look at something to make sure it is right.
entries.exampleSentence: Please check your work before you finish.

entries.englishDefinition: Whole, or to finish something.
entries.exampleSentence: I complete a task before dinner every night.

entries.englishDefinition: To keep doing something.
entries.exampleSentence: Please continue to read the story after lunch.

entries.englishDefinition: Something on top, or to put something over another thing.
entries.exampleSentence: Cover the box with a cloth before painting.

entries.englishDefinition: To tell what someone or something is like.
entries.exampleSentence: Please describe the picture in detail to your partner.

entries.englishDefinition: To make something clear or easy to understand.
entries.exampleSentence: Can you explain the game to me?

entries.englishDefinition: To go into a place.
entries.exampleSentence: Please enter the room quietly before the lesson starts.

entries.englishDefinition: To make something full.
entries.exampleSentence: Fill the bottle with water before soccer practice.

entries.englishDefinition: To get to a place, point, or goal.
entries.exampleSentence: Can you reach for the book on the shelf?

entries.englishDefinition: To get something from someone.
entries.exampleSentence: I received a letter from my friend.

entries.englishDefinition: To go or come back.
entries.exampleSentence: We return to school after lunch every Monday.

entries.englishDefinition: To say or do something again.
entries.exampleSentence: Please repeat after me during English reading practice.

entries.englishDefinition: To help someone remember something.
entries.exampleSentence: Please remind me to bring my book.

entries.englishDefinition: To try to find someone or something.
entries.exampleSentence: I search for my missing notebook after school.

entries.englishDefinition: To move things apart or keep them apart.
entries.exampleSentence: Please separate the red cards from the blue cards.

entries.englishDefinition: To use time or money for something.
entries.exampleSentence: I spend time with my family every Sunday.

entries.englishDefinition: To tell or write information about something.
entries.exampleSentence: We write a report about animals in class.

entries.englishDefinition: To hit your hands together to show you like something.
entries.exampleSentence: The students clap for the singer after the song.

entries.englishDefinition: An instruction people should follow, or to control.
entries.exampleSentence: We follow the rule to keep everyone safe.

entries.englishDefinition: To go from one place to another place.
entries.exampleSentence: My family will travel to Japan next summer.

entries.englishDefinition: To hope for something you want.
entries.exampleSentence: I wish for a new bike on my birthday.

entries.englishDefinition: To do a job or activity.
entries.exampleSentence: We work on the project after lunch today.

entries.englishDefinition: To push air out of your throat with a sound.
entries.exampleSentence: I have a cough and need some warm water.

entries.englishDefinition: A pain in your head.
entries.exampleSentence: I have a headache after reading for too long.

entries.englishDefinition: To show something to others, or a gift.
entries.exampleSentence: She will present her drawing to the class today.

entries.englishDefinition: To plan when something will happen.
entries.exampleSentence: We schedule a meeting with our teacher tomorrow.

entries.englishDefinition: Sleeping, not awake.
entries.exampleSentence: I fall asleep after reading my favorite story.

entries.englishDefinition: Not sleeping.
entries.exampleSentence: I stay awake during the exciting movie tonight.

entries.englishDefinition: Ready to do something difficult or scary.
entries.exampleSentence: She is brave enough to speak on stage.

entries.englishDefinition: Feeling good and relaxed.
entries.exampleSentence: This chair is comfortable enough for long reading.

entries.englishDefinition: Wanting to know or learn about something.
entries.exampleSentence: I am curious about how plants grow bigger.

entries.englishDefinition: Not open.
entries.exampleSentence: The library is closed during the school holiday.

entries.englishDefinition: Coming at the end, or the act of shutting.
entries.exampleSentence: The closing song makes everyone smile at school.

entries.englishDefinition: Not easy to do or understand.
entries.exampleSentence: This math problem is difficult for many students today.

entries.englishDefinition: Not the same as another thing.
entries.exampleSentence: My answer is different from your answer today.

entries.englishDefinition: Not safe; able to hurt someone.
entries.exampleSentence: It is dangerous to run across the road.

entries.englishDefinition: Different from normal, or important to someone.
entries.exampleSentence: This card is special to my little sister.

entries.englishDefinition: No longer living or working.
entries.exampleSentence: The plant is dead because it had no water.

entries.englishDefinition: Kind and nice to other people.
entries.exampleSentence: Our new neighbor is friendly to everyone here.

entries.englishDefinition: Done or completed.
entries.exampleSentence: I am finished with my homework before dinner.

entries.englishDefinition: Completely different, or across from something.
entries.exampleSentence: The bus stop is opposite the school gate.

entries.englishDefinition: Not joking, or important and needing care.
entries.exampleSentence: Please be serious about this important safety rule.

entries.englishDefinition: Not making any sound.
entries.exampleSentence: The room is silent during the reading test.

entries.englishDefinition: A pain in your stomach.
entries.exampleSentence: I have a stomachache after eating too quickly.

entries.englishDefinition: Not strong.
entries.exampleSentence: I feel weak after running for a long time.

entries.englishDefinition: Without other people.
entries.exampleSentence: I stay alone in the classroom after school.

entries.englishDefinition: Full of light or easy to see.
entries.exampleSentence: The room is bright in the morning sun.

entries.englishDefinition: Showing love and care.
entries.exampleSentence: My grandmother is loving to every child here.

entries.englishDefinition: Right and equal for everyone.
entries.exampleSentence: The teacher is fair to all students.

entries.englishDefinition: Very good.
entries.exampleSentence: She did an excellent job on the poster.

entries.englishDefinition: Very happy and interested about something.
entries.exampleSentence: I am excited about the school trip tomorrow.

entries.englishDefinition: Making people feel very interested or happy.
entries.exampleSentence: The game is exciting for everyone in class.

entries.englishDefinition: Not wise or not serious.
entries.exampleSentence: It is silly to wear socks on your hands.

entries.englishDefinition: Having four equal sides and four corners.
entries.exampleSentence: The window has a square shape in the picture.

entries.englishDefinition: Shaped like a circle or a ball.
entries.exampleSentence: We sit at the round table during art class.

entries.englishDefinition: Happy or pleased about something.
entries.exampleSentence: I am glad to help my friend after school.

entries.englishDefinition: A long distance away from a place.
entries.exampleSentence: My home is far from school on rainy days.

entries.englishDefinition: Very strange, wild, or full of energy.
entries.exampleSentence: The class had a crazy idea for the show.

entries.englishDefinition: Not agreeing with someone or facing something.
entries.exampleSentence: Our team played against the other class today.

entries.englishDefinition: Moving beside or following the length of something.
entries.exampleSentence: We walked along the river after lunch today.

entries.englishDefinition: Not including someone or something.
entries.exampleSentence: Everyone came to practice except Tom and Lisa.

entries.englishDefinition: From a time in the past until now.
entries.exampleSentence: I have liked music since I was little.

entries.englishDefinition: One or the other of two choices.
entries.exampleSentence: You can choose either one for the game.

entries.englishDefinition: Belonging to a girl or woman already mentioned.
entries.exampleSentence: This blue notebook is hers, not mine.

entries.englishDefinition: Belonging to me.
entries.exampleSentence: That red pencil is mine, so please return it.

entries.englishDefinition: Not anything; no thing.
entries.exampleSentence: There is nothing to do before the bell rings.

entries.englishDefinition: Not one of a group or amount.
entries.exampleSentence: None of them wanted to miss the school trip.

entries.englishDefinition: No person; not anyone.
entries.exampleSentence: Nobody in our group forgot the homework today.

entries.meaning_zh: 我們的（名詞性物主代詞）
entries.englishDefinition: Belonging to us.
entries.exampleSentence: The clean classroom is ours after we decorated it.

entries.englishDefinition: We or us without help from other people.
entries.exampleSentence: We made the poster by ourselves after class.

entries.englishDefinition: A person, but you do not say who.
entries.exampleSentence: Somebody left a lunch box near the door.

entries.englishDefinition: A person, but you do not know or say who.
entries.exampleSentence: Someone called my name across the playground today.

entries.englishDefinition: A thing, idea, or event that is not named.
entries.exampleSentence: I want something warm to drink after swimming.

entries.englishDefinition: Belonging to you.
entries.exampleSentence: This seat is yours if you want it.

entries.englishDefinition: You without help from another person.
entries.exampleSentence: You can finish the puzzle by yourself today.

entries.englishDefinition: Used for a girl or woman as the object, or to show belonging.
entries.exampleSentence: I gave her a pencil during English class.

entries.englishDefinition: Used for a boy or man as the object.
entries.exampleSentence: The teacher asked him to read the sentence.

entries.englishDefinition: Belonging to an animal, thing, or place.
entries.exampleSentence: The dog wagged its tail when we came home.

entries.englishDefinition: Used when someone is talking about the speaker as the object.
entries.exampleSentence: My sister helped me carry the heavy bag.

entries.englishDefinition: Belonging to us.
entries.exampleSentence: Our class cleaned the library before lunch today.

entries.englishDefinition: Belonging to them.
entries.exampleSentence: Their team practiced hard before the final game.

entries.englishDefinition: Used for people or things as the object.
entries.exampleSentence: I helped them carry boxes to the classroom.

entries.englishDefinition: The people or things near us now.
entries.exampleSentence: These books belong on the top shelf.

entries.englishDefinition: Used to talk about people, animals, or things already mentioned.
entries.exampleSentence: They arrived early for the science fair today.

entries.englishDefinition: The people or things farther away from us.
entries.exampleSentence: Those shoes by the door are not mine.

entries.englishDefinition: Used for we as the object.
entries.exampleSentence: The coach taught us a new game today.

entries.englishDefinition: Used when the speaker talks about a group including themselves.
entries.exampleSentence: We worked together to finish the project early.

entries.englishDefinition: Used to ask or say which person something belongs to.
entries.exampleSentence: Whose bag is on the chair near the window?

entries.englishDefinition: Any thing, often used in questions or negative sentences.
entries.exampleSentence: Do you need anything else before we leave?

entries.englishDefinition: Any person, often used in questions or negative sentences.
entries.exampleSentence: Can anybody help me move this table?

entries.englishDefinition: Any person; anybody.
entries.exampleSentence: Did anyone see my ruler after art class?

entries.englishDefinition: Every person in a group.
entries.exampleSentence: Everybody listened carefully during the safety lesson today.

entries.englishDefinition: Every person; everybody.
entries.exampleSentence: Everyone brought a notebook to English class today.

entries.englishDefinition: All things in a group or situation.
entries.exampleSentence: Everything in my desk is neat and ready.

entries.englishDefinition: In, at, or to every place.
entries.exampleSentence: We looked everywhere for the missing library card.

entries.englishDefinition: She or her without help from another person.
entries.exampleSentence: She finished the drawing by herself after dinner.

entries.englishDefinition: He or him without help from another person.
entries.exampleSentence: He fixed the model by himself at home.

entries.englishDefinition: I or me without help from another person.
entries.exampleSentence: I packed my school bag by myself today.

entries.englishDefinition: Completely or very strongly.
entries.exampleSentence: I am absolutely sure about my answer today.

entries.englishDefinition: At a later time; after something happens.
entries.exampleSentence: We ate lunch and played basketball afterwards outside.

entries.englishDefinition: Nearly, but not completely.
entries.exampleSentence: I almost finished my homework before dinner tonight.

entries.englishDefinition: Before now or before a certain time.
entries.exampleSentence: She has already read that story twice this week.

entries.englishDefinition: In front or forward in space or time.
entries.exampleSentence: Please walk ahead of me on the path.

entries.englishDefinition: In every way or as much as possible.
entries.exampleSentence: The room was completely quiet during the test today.

entries.englishDefinition: In another way, not the same way.
entries.exampleSentence: We solved the math problem differently this time.

entries.englishDefinition: Other or more than the thing mentioned.
entries.exampleSentence: Do you want anything else for your lunch?

entries.englishDefinition: In an honest and true way.
entries.exampleSentence: She sincerely thanked her teacher after the concert.

entries.englishDefinition: Quickly and without warning.
entries.exampleSentence: The lights suddenly went out during the storm.

entries.englishDefinition: Up to now, or still not at this time.
entries.exampleSentence: I have not finished my reading homework yet.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who makes bread, cakes, or cookies.
entries.exampleSentence: The baker made fresh bread early this morning.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who writes books, stories, or articles.
entries.exampleSentence: The writer shared a funny story with our class.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who does a job or task.
entries.exampleSentence: The worker fixed the broken chair after school.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who sings songs.
entries.exampleSentence: The singer practiced a new song before dinner.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who flies an airplane.
entries.exampleSentence: The pilot landed the airplane safely at night.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who helps sick or hurt people.
entries.exampleSentence: The doctor checked my throat at the clinic.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who grows food or takes care of farm animals.
entries.exampleSentence: The farmer grows vegetables near the small town.

entries.englishDefinition: A man or boy who acts in a play, movie, or show.
entries.exampleSentence: The actor remembered every line in the school play.

entries.englishDefinition: A woman or girl who acts in a play, movie, or show.
entries.exampleSentence: The actress smiled brightly after the final scene.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who makes art, such as pictures or music.
entries.exampleSentence: The artist painted a bright picture for school.

entries.englishDefinition: A person whose job is cooking food.
entries.exampleSentence: The chef cooked vegetable soup for the students.

entries.englishDefinition: A person whose job is taking care of teeth.
entries.exampleSentence: The dentist cleaned my teeth after lunch today.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who drives a car, bus, or truck.
entries.exampleSentence: The driver stopped the bus near our school.

entries.englishDefinition: A person whose job is stopping fires and helping people.
entries.exampleSentence: The firefighter taught us about safety at school.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who works in a library.
entries.exampleSentence: The librarian helped me find a science book.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who helps doctors care for sick people.
entries.exampleSentence: The nurse gave me water after the checkup.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who studies science and learns how things work.
entries.exampleSentence: The scientist tested the water in the classroom.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who dances for work or practice.
entries.exampleSentence: The dancer moved gracefully across the stage tonight.

entries.englishDefinition: Used to join words or ideas together.
entries.exampleSentence: I packed pencils and notebooks for school today.

entries.englishDefinition: Used to compare things or show a role.
entries.exampleSentence: She dressed as a doctor for career day.

entries.englishDefinition: Used to give a reason.
entries.exampleSentence: I stayed home because I had a fever.

entries.englishDefinition: Used to show a different or opposite idea.
entries.exampleSentence: The question looked easy, but I read carefully.

entries.englishDefinition: Used to talk about something that may happen.
entries.exampleSentence: If it rains tomorrow, we will stay inside.

entries.englishDefinition: Used to show a choice between things.
entries.exampleSentence: Would you like milk or juice with lunch?

entries.englishDefinition: During the time that something is happening.
entries.exampleSentence: I listened to music while I cleaned my room.

entries.englishDefinition: The earth and all the people and places on it.
entries.exampleSentence: Children around the world learn many different languages.

entries.englishDefinition: A large country and continent in the Southern Hemisphere.
entries.exampleSentence: My cousin visited Australia during her summer vacation.

entries.englishDefinition: A large continent with many countries.
entries.exampleSentence: Africa has many countries, languages, and beautiful places.

entries.englishDefinition: The largest continent in the world.
entries.exampleSentence: Many students in Asia study English at school.

entries.englishDefinition: From or related to Asia or its people.
entries.exampleSentence: We learned about Asian food during culture week.

entries.englishDefinition: A continent with many countries such as France and Germany.
entries.exampleSentence: Europe has many old cities and famous museums.

entries.englishDefinition: The United States, or the continents of North and South America.
entries.exampleSentence: My aunt lives in America with her family.

entries.englishDefinition: A country in Europe known for Paris.
entries.exampleSentence: France is famous for art, bread, and museums.

entries.englishDefinition: A country in Europe known for Berlin.
entries.exampleSentence: Germany has many castles, forests, and busy cities.

entries.englishDefinition: A country in Asia made of many islands.
entries.exampleSentence: Japan has beautiful cherry blossoms in the spring.

entries.englishDefinition: A large country in Asia.
entries.exampleSentence: India has many languages, festivals, and colorful clothes.

entries.englishDefinition: The United Kingdom, a country made of four parts.
entries.exampleSentence: The UK has England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

entries.englishDefinition: The United States, a country in North America.
entries.exampleSentence: The U.S. has many states and famous cities.

entries.englishDefinition: Another name often used for Great Britain.
entries.exampleSentence: Britain has many old castles and busy towns.

entries.englishDefinition: A large country in Asia.
entries.exampleSentence: China has many mountains, rivers, and big cities.

entries.englishDefinition: A large country in North America.
entries.exampleSentence: Canada has cold winters and many beautiful lakes.

entries.englishDefinition: A country that is part of the United Kingdom.
entries.exampleSentence: England is famous for London and rainy weather.

entries.englishDefinition: A very large country in Europe and Asia.
entries.exampleSentence: Russia has long winters and many large forests.

entries.englishDefinition: An area of land with its own government and people.
entries.exampleSentence: Each country has its own flag and language.

entries.englishDefinition: From France, or the language spoken in France.
entries.exampleSentence: We learned a French song in music class.

entries.englishDefinition: From Germany, or the language spoken in Germany.
entries.exampleSentence: My friend learned a German word yesterday.

entries.englishDefinition: From India, or related to India.
entries.exampleSentence: We tasted Indian food during culture week.

entries.englishDefinition: From Japan, or the language spoken in Japan.
entries.exampleSentence: She wrote a Japanese word on the board.

entries.englishDefinition: From Russia, or the language spoken in Russia.
entries.exampleSentence: We saw a Russian dance in the video.

entries.englishDefinition: A person from the United States.
entries.exampleSentence: An American student visited our class today.

entries.englishDefinition: From Australia, or related to Australia.
entries.exampleSentence: The Australian flag was on the classroom wall.

entries.englishDefinition: From Britain, or related to Britain.
entries.exampleSentence: The British teacher told us a funny story.

entries.englishDefinition: From China, or the language spoken in China.
entries.exampleSentence: We practiced Chinese writing after lunch today.

entries.englishDefinition: From Canada, or related to Canada.
entries.exampleSentence: A Canadian family moved near our school.

entries.englishDefinition: The language used in this lesson, or related to England.
entries.exampleSentence: English class starts right after math today.

entries.englishDefinition: From Africa, or related to Africa.
entries.exampleSentence: We learned about African music in class.

entries.englishDefinition: A small cloth bag in clothes for carrying things.
entries.exampleSentence: I put my key in my jacket pocket.

entries.englishDefinition: A location or area where something is.
entries.exampleSentence: This quiet place is good for reading books.

entries.englishDefinition: A machine that receives sound programs and music.
entries.exampleSentence: Grandpa listens to the radio every morning.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who reads books, stories, or articles.
entries.exampleSentence: A good reader asks questions while reading.

entries.englishDefinition: The activity of looking at and understanding words.
entries.exampleSentence: Reading before bed helps me learn new words.

entries.englishDefinition: A small room or space for storing clothes and things.
entries.exampleSentence: I put my coat inside the closet.

entries.englishDefinition: A long hard path for cars and buses.
entries.exampleSentence: The road near our school is very busy.

entries.englishDefinition: The top cover of a building or house.
entries.exampleSentence: Rain fell loudly on the metal roof.

entries.englishDefinition: A straight line of people or things.
entries.exampleSentence: We sat in a row during the concert.

entries.englishDefinition: A small round sound, or to make a bell sound.
entries.exampleSentence: The school bell will ring after lunch.

entries.englishDefinition: A group of things that belong together.
entries.exampleSentence: I bought a set of colored pencils.

entries.englishDefinition: A chair or place where someone can sit.
entries.exampleSentence: Please take a seat near the window.

entries.englishDefinition: A thick liquid put on food for flavor.
entries.exampleSentence: She put tomato sauce on her noodles.

entries.englishDefinition: A white substance used to make food taste better.
entries.exampleSentence: Dad added a little salt to the soup.

entries.englishDefinition: A large piece of cloth used on a bed.
entries.exampleSentence: Mom changed the bed sheet on Sunday.

entries.englishDefinition: The left, right, front, or back part of something.
entries.exampleSentence: Write your name on the left side.

entries.englishDefinition: A mark, picture, or words that give information.
entries.exampleSentence: The sign says we should walk slowly.

entries.englishDefinition: A game or activity that uses body movement and skill.
entries.exampleSentence: Basketball is my favorite sport after school.

entries.englishDefinition: One step in a set of steps.
entries.exampleSentence: Be careful on the wet stair today.

entries.englishDefinition: A small paper mark put on a letter.
entries.exampleSentence: I put a stamp on the postcard.

entries.englishDefinition: One action in a process, or one movement of a foot.
entries.exampleSentence: The first step is to wash your hands.

entries.englishDefinition: Very bad weather with strong wind, rain, or snow.
entries.exampleSentence: The storm kept everyone inside after school.

entries.englishDefinition: The activity of moving through water with your body.
entries.exampleSentence: Swimming is fun when the water is warm.

entries.englishDefinition: The back part of an animal body.
entries.exampleSentence: The dog wagged its tail near the door.

entries.englishDefinition: A car you pay to ride in.
entries.exampleSentence: We took a taxi to the airport early.

entries.englishDefinition: A liquid soap used for washing hair.
entries.exampleSentence: I need shampoo to wash my hair tonight.

entries.englishDefinition: The act of choosing between two or more things.
entries.exampleSentence: You made a good choice for lunch today.

entries.englishDefinition: A group of people who work or play together.
entries.exampleSentence: Our team practiced hard before the game.

entries.englishDefinition: A device used to talk to someone far away.
entries.exampleSentence: I called Grandma on the telephone after dinner.

entries.englishDefinition: A machine with a screen for watching shows.
entries.exampleSentence: We watched a nature show on television.

entries.englishDefinition: A sport played with rackets and a ball.
entries.exampleSentence: My brother plays tennis every Saturday morning.

entries.englishDefinition: A seat used in a bathroom.
entries.exampleSentence: Please clean the toilet after you use it.

entries.englishDefinition: The highest part or surface of something.
entries.exampleSentence: Put the box on the top shelf.

entries.englishDefinition: A piece of cloth used for drying things.
entries.exampleSentence: I used a towel after swimming class.

entries.englishDefinition: A place where people live, smaller than a city.
entries.exampleSentence: Our town has a library and park.

entries.englishDefinition: A raised line of water moving across the surface.
entries.exampleSentence: A big wave came toward the beach.

entries.englishDefinition: Saturday and Sunday, or the end of the week.
entries.exampleSentence: We visit my grandparents on the weekend.

entries.englishDefinition: A structure built over water, roads, or valleys.
entries.exampleSentence: The bus crossed the bridge after school.

entries.englishDefinition: A tool with hairs used for cleaning or painting.
entries.exampleSentence: Use a brush to clean your shoes.

entries.englishDefinition: A container used for carrying or holding things.
entries.exampleSentence: She carried apples in a small basket.

entries.englishDefinition: A piece of furniture used for holding books.
entries.exampleSentence: The dictionary is on the bookcase shelf.

entries.englishDefinition: One of the four parts of the year, or a time period.
entries.exampleSentence: Spring is my favorite season of the year.

entries.englishDefinition: An opening in a wall with glass for light and air.
entries.exampleSentence: Please open the window before class starts.

entries.englishDefinition: A place where airplanes take off and land.
entries.exampleSentence: We arrived at the airport before sunrise.

entries.englishDefinition: A flying vehicle with wings and engines.
entries.exampleSentence: The airplane flew above the clouds today.

entries.englishDefinition: The season after summer and before winter.
entries.exampleSentence: Leaves turn red and yellow in autumn.

entries.englishDefinition: A chart that shows days, weeks, and months.
entries.exampleSentence: Mark the test date on your calendar.

entries.englishDefinition: A plate or a kind of food.
entries.exampleSentence: This dish has rice, vegetables, and chicken.

entries.englishDefinition: Things that happened in the past.
entries.exampleSentence: We studied ancient history in class today.

entries.englishDefinition: A place where sick or hurt people get care.
entries.exampleSentence: My uncle works at the hospital near school.

entries.englishDefinition: A period of sixty minutes.
entries.exampleSentence: The movie lasted one hour after dinner.

entries.englishDefinition: A married man in relation to his wife.
entries.exampleSentence: Her husband cooked dinner for the family.

entries.englishDefinition: A place where people pay to sleep when traveling.
entries.exampleSentence: We stayed at a hotel near the station.

entries.englishDefinition: A printed paper with news and information.
entries.exampleSentence: Dad reads the newspaper after breakfast every morning.

entries.englishDefinition: A person who lives near you.
entries.exampleSentence: Our neighbour helped us carry the boxes.

entries.englishDefinition: A piece of material with holes used to catch or hold things.
entries.exampleSentence: The ball hit the net during tennis practice.

entries.englishDefinition: A home that a bird makes for its eggs.
entries.exampleSentence: We saw a small nest in the tree.

entries.englishDefinition: Twelve o clock in the middle of the day.
entries.exampleSentence: We eat lunch at noon after English class.

enrichment.note: 原始欄位保留不改；補充欄位用於前端練習卡與未來重新生成 vocabulary.js。
